findings.evidence: `rg -n "\@\[expose\] public section|noncomputable def weightedBasepointChangeClass|noncomputable def unweightedBasepointChangeClass"` shows the exposed section at line 34 and the new public definitions at lines 52 and 135, while lines 58 and 139 already provide the coercion `[simp]` lemmas.
findings.fix: Use a plain `public section` here and keep bodies hidden; if a downstream proof needs unfolding, add the missing characteristic lemma instead of exposing the definition.
findings.issue: `@[expose] public section` exposes the bodies of the new public definitions such as `weightedBasepointChangeClass` and `unweightedBasepointChangeClass`; this makes downstream code able to unfold the implementation instead of using the API.

summary: The new basepoint-change API exposes implementation bodies even though it also provides the characteristic coercion and change-base lemmas needed by downstream users.
